summary: Matched Falco's fighter-character file by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.s with src/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.c and a new ftfalco.h header. The C implementation covers Falco OnDeath/OnLoad plus several item/visibility/part-animation helper functions, updates the build to use the C object, adds ftfalco.o to the epilogue-unscheduled list, and types func_800E5858 in include/functions.h. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the provided context, so intent beyond matching ftfalco is inferred from the diff only.
what_changed: Deleted the 268-line hand-written/disassembled assembly file asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.c with C implementations for ftFalco_OnDeath, ftFalco_OnLoad, and seven address-named helper functions from the deleted assembly.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.h with prototypes and includes for HSD_GObj and Fighter definitions.
Changed obj_files.mk so ftfalco.o is built from src/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.o.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftfalco.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Changed include/functions.h declaration for func_800E5858 from an unknown commented placeholder to void func_800E5858(struct _HSD_GObj*).
Reconstructed ftFalco_OnDeath as clearing Fighter.x222C and calling func_80074A4C(gobj, 0, 0).
Reconstructed ftFalco_OnLoad as setting ft->x2224_flag.bits.b7, calling func_800E576C(ft), then initializing item data with func_8026B3F8 for items[0], items[1], and items[3].
Reconstructed helper logic around held items via ft->x1974_heldItem, func_8026B2B4, func_8026B320, func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, and func_80070CC4.
Reconstructed helper logic applying part animation and visibility/scale-like state through func_800704F0 and ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim using float constants 3.0f and 0.0f.
smart_moves: Kept unresolved/unknown functions address-named where no stronger semantic names were evident, avoiding speculative renaming.
Introduced a dedicated ftfalco.h header so the newly matched C file exposes only the functions it implements and can be included consistently by future Falco work.

Used existing Fighter and ftData struct fields such as user_data, x10C_ftData, x48_items, x2224_flag.bits.b7, x2D4_specialAttributes, x1974_heldItem, and x222C instead of raw offsets from the assembly.
Converted the assembly switch-like compare/branch ladder in func_80149B00 into a clear C switch over func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), preserving only the handled cases 1 through 4.
Matched small wrapper functions directly, including func_80149D3C as a direct call to func_800E5858(gobj), which also justified adding the typed prototype.
Represented .sdata2 constants as ordinary float literals in C, replacing lbl_804D9DB0 with 3.0f and lbl_804D9DB4 with 0.0f.
decomp_lessons: When converting character files from asm to C, remember to update both obj_files.mk and e_files.mk if the object participates in epilogue-unscheduled ordering.
Assembly loads from gobj offset 0x2C can often become HSD_GObj.user_data cast to Fighter*, enabling use of known Fighter field names instead of raw offsets.
Bit writes through packed Fighter flags can be expressed via named bitfields, as with ft->x2224_flag.bits.b7 = 1.
Special attributes accessed by byte offsets can sometimes be modeled as an indexed pointer after assigning ft->x2D4_specialAttributes; here offsets 0x1C and 0x20 became sa2[7] and sa2[8].
A branch ladder around small integer return values may match cleanly as a switch only when default behavior is a no-op and handled cases map directly to calls.
Leaving intentionally unused locals such as unused variables can be part of preserving compiler behavior for matching, though the exact reason is not documented in the PR text.
Thin wrappers around existing engine functions are still useful matches because they remove assembly files and can reveal missing function prototypes.
naming_conventions: Character lifecycle functions use mixed-case fighter names with OnDeath and OnLoad suffixes, e.g. ftFalco_OnDeath and ftFalco_OnLoad.
Unidentified functions retain address-derived names such as func_80149B00, func_80149BE0, func_80149C28, func_80149C70, func_80149D3C, func_80149D5C, and func_80149DC8.
The new header uses the project-local lowercase filename style ftfalco.h and include guard _ftfalco_h_.
Existing struct member names preserve offset-style prefixes where full semantics are still unclear, e.g. x222C, x1974_heldItem, x10C_ftData, x48_items, x2224_flag, and x2D4_specialAttributes.
Temporary decomp variables are descriptive only when evidence supports it, such as result, switched_result, ftdata, items, and sa2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the entire ftfalco.s after producing a matching C translation and redirected the object list from asm to src.
Translated lbl_804D9DB0 0x40400000 to 3.0f and lbl_804D9DB4 0x00000000 to 0.0f in the C calls.
Preserved call ordering in ftFalco_OnLoad: set flag, call func_800E576C, then initialize three item-related entries with func_8026B3F8.
Used explicit s32 parameter types for helpers taking integer flags to mirror the assembly's integer branch checks.
Kept some local declarations, including unused locals, likely to influence stack/register allocation for matching; this is inferred from the diff because no comments explain them.
Converted repeated assembly sequences that conditionally call func_80070C48 or func_80070CC4 into small C functions gated by func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0.

Represented paired part-animation calls exactly: func_80149D5C applies part anim state 3 to parts 3 and 4 after setting slots 1 and 0 to 3.0f, while func_80149DC8 applies state 2 after setting those slots to 0.0f.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; observations are based on the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document Falco helper functions func_80149B00 through func_80149DC8.
Search for other fighter character OnLoad implementations that initialize item resources with func_8026B3F8.
Search for uses of x2224_flag.bits.b7 in other character files to infer its semantic name.
Search for func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, and func_80070CC4 call patterns in Fox or Falco files to identify their purpose.
Check later headers for whether func_800E5858 or func_800E576C received semantic names or better prototypes.
